proposed_hypotheses.text: Pearson correlation between feature_078 (continuous, range 30-90) and pfs_months is non-zero, with the direction to be determined empirically.
Among 77 binary features, at least one shows a non-zero mean difference in pfs_months between its 0 and 1 strata.
Among 10 ordinal/categorical features, at least one shows a monotonic Spearman association with pfs_months.
Mean pfs_months differs across levels of the categorical feature_018 (race: asian/black/hispanic/other/white).
Mean pfs_months differs across levels of the categorical feature_045 (insurance: medicaid/medicare/private/uninsured).

analyses.code:
from scipy.stats import pearsonr
for c in cont_features: pearsonr(df[c], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: feature_078 shows the strongest continuous correlation with pfs_months (r=+0.856, p<<1e-300). Other notable correlations: feature_013 r=-0.153 (p=3.5e-260), feature_006 r=-0.143 (p=9.9e-226), feature_009 r=+0.123 (p=1.5e-166). Remaining continuous features have |r|<0.02.

analyses.code: for c in binary_features: ttest_ind(df.loc[df[c]==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df[c]==0,'pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: feature_051 (prevalence 0.55) shows the largest binary effect: mean PFS 4.03 (off) vs 3.51 (on), diff -0.521 months, p=1.6e-181. Two other binaries (feature_109, feature_039) had positive effects of +0.1 month (p~0.002-0.003).

analyses.code: for c in ordinal_features: spearmanr(df[c], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: feature_057 (ordinal 0/1/2) shows a strong monotonic negative association with pfs_months (Spearman rho=-0.386, p<<1e-300). All other ordinal features (feature_067 Gleason-like, feature_016, feature_071, feature_026, feature_096, feature_033, feature_064) showed |rho|<0.01 with p>0.25.
One-way ANOVA across the 5 race categories: F=0.56, p=0.689. Mean PFS by race ranges 3.74 (white/black/hispanic) to 3.80 (asian) -- not significant.
One-way ANOVA across the 4 insurance categories: F=0.34, p=0.794. Mean PFS spans 3.73 (uninsured) to 3.75 (medicare) -- no insurance disparity detected unadjusted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_078 is associated with longer pfs_months in linear regression, with a positive slope close to its univariable estimate.
feature_051=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months by approximately -0.5 months in a 3-feature linear model adjusted for feature_078 and feature_057.
Each one-step increase in feature_057 (0->1->2) is associated with shorter pfs_months in a monotonic, roughly linear fashion (about -1.1 months per step).
feature_078, feature_051, and feature_057 are not strongly mutually confounded -- their joint linear coefficients each remain similar to their univariable estimates.

analyses.code:
import statsmodels.api as sm
X = sm.add_constant(df[['feature_078','feature_051','feature_057']])
m = sm.OLS(df['pfs_months'], X).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Joint OLS coefficients: feature_078 +0.1737/unit (t=592, p~0); feature_051 -0.520 (t=-89, p~0); feature_057 -1.150/step (t=-267, p~0). R^2=0.896. Each coefficient is essentially identical to its univariable estimate, so the three predictors are not mutually confounded.

Mean PFS: feature_057=0 -> 4.68 (n=17,592), =1 -> 3.49 (n=24,971), =2 -> 2.38 (n=7,437). Differences are nearly equal: -1.19 (0 vs 1, p~0) and -1.12 (1 vs 2, p~0), confirming monotonic, near-linear decline.

proposed_hypotheses.text: log1p(feature_013) (PSA-like, highly skewed) is independently associated with shorter pfs_months after adjusting for feature_078, feature_051, feature_057.
feature_006 is independently associated with shorter pfs_months after adjusting for feature_078, feature_051, feature_057.
feature_009 is independently associated with longer pfs_months after adjusting for feature_078, feature_051, feature_057.
feature_092 retains a small but statistically significant negative coefficient on pfs_months after adjustment.
Other modestly correlated continuous features (feature_038, feature_014, feature_010, feature_101, feature_099) lose significance after adjustment for the top-3 base.

analyses.result_summary: log_f013 adjusted coefficient = -0.385 per natural-log unit (t=-168, p~0). Strong independent negative effect.
feature_006 adjusted coefficient = -0.0768/unit (t=-114, p~0). Independent negative effect.
feature_009 adjusted coefficient = +0.487/unit (t=90, p~0). Independent positive effect.
feature_092 adjusted coefficient = -0.00050/unit (t=-14.3, p=3.3e-46). Small but highly significant negative effect.

feature_038 (p=0.68), log feature_014 (p=0.11), log feature_010 (p=0.58), log feature_101 (p=0.87), and log feature_099 (p=0.84) lose significance after adjustment, confirming their univariable correlations were confounded by the base predictors.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for case-mix (feature_078, feature_051, feature_057, log feature_013, feature_006, feature_009), pfs_months does not differ across feature_018 race categories.
After adjusting for case-mix, pfs_months does not differ across feature_045 insurance categories.
feature_051 prevalence does not differ across racial groups (no apparent treatment-allocation disparity by race).
feature_057 (severity) distribution does not differ across racial groups.
feature_051 prevalence does not differ across insurance groups.

analyses.code: OLS with race dummies on top of 6-feature base model
analyses.result_summary: All race-category coefficients vs. baseline are |beta|<=0.003 with p>=0.67 (black p=0.77, hispanic p=0.83, white p=0.67, other p=1.00). No adjusted racial difference in PFS.

analyses.code: OLS with insurance dummies on top of 6-feature base model
analyses.result_summary: All insurance coefficients have |beta|<=0.001 with p>=0.90. No adjusted insurance disparity in PFS.
Race x feature_051: chi^2=1.55, dof=4, p=0.818. feature_051 prevalence ranges 0.544 (other/hispanic) to 0.554 (black) -- balanced across races.
Race x feature_057: chi^2=4.15, dof=8, p=0.843. Mean feature_057 ranges 0.79-0.81 across races -- balanced.
Insurance x feature_051: chi^2=3.13, dof=3, p=0.372. Prevalence 0.55-0.56 across all insurance categories.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_051=1 is associated with substantially higher feature_013 (PSA-like) values, explaining why the feature_051 effect is mediated by log feature_013.
After controlling for feature_078, feature_057, log feature_013, feature_006, and feature_009, feature_051 is no longer a significant predictor of pfs_months.
Beyond the 6-feature base, feature_109 has a significant positive residual association with pfs_months.
Beyond the 6-feature base, feature_039 has a significant positive residual association with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_013: 11.96 (feature_051=0) vs 67.83 (feature_051=1), difference +55.87 (t=77, p~0). On log1p scale: +1.38 log units (t=155, p~0). feature_051 is strongly tied to elevated feature_013, which mediates its univariable PFS effect.
feature_051 adjusted coefficient = +0.0006 (SE 0.004), p=0.87. Effect fully attenuated -- the apparent prognostic value of feature_051 is mediated by feature_013.

analyses.code: Pearson correlation of OLS residuals from 6-feature model against each remaining feature.
analyses.result_summary: feature_109 (prevalence 0.10): r=+0.129 with residuals (p=2e-183). feature_039 (prevalence 0.10): r=+0.126 (p=6e-176). feature_092: r=-0.118 (p=3e-154). Both feature_109 and feature_039 are positively associated with longer PFS independently of the base, with similar magnitude.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In an 8-feature multivariable model adding feature_109, feature_039, and feature_092, all three contribute significantly with positive (109, 039) and small negative (092) coefficients.
feature_109 and feature_039 are not mutually exclusive (some patients have both = 1), indicating they are not coded as a treatment partition.
There is no significant interaction between feature_109 and feature_057 (treatment effect does not vary across severity strata).
There is no significant interaction between feature_039 and feature_057.
The marginal effect of feature_109 is larger in feature_051=0 patients than in feature_051=1 patients.

analyses.result_summary: Of 50,000 patients: 40,435 have neither, 4,569 have only feature_039, 4,467 only feature_109, and 529 have both. They are not coded as a partition; their joint distribution suggests independent indicators.

analyses.code: OLS with feature_109 * feature_057 product term
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient = +0.0051 (SE 0.0076), p=0.51. No interaction.

analyses.code: OLS with feature_039 * feature_057 product term
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient = +0.0044 (SE 0.0076), p=0.56. No interaction.

analyses.code: Stratify by feature_051 and ttest feature_109
analyses.result_summary: feature_051=0: feature_109 effect +0.141 months (p=3.4e-3, n1=2245). feature_051=1: feature_109 effect +0.062 (p=0.15, n1=2751). Numerically larger in feature_051=0, but the difference between strata is not formally tested as significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: log1p(feature_013) interacts positively with feature_057: the negative slope of log feature_013 on pfs_months is attenuated (less steep) at higher severity.
feature_078 interacts negatively with feature_057: the positive age slope is attenuated at higher severity (i.e., older patients with high feature_057 do worse than the additive model predicts).
feature_109 effect on pfs_months is consistent across the 5 race categories of feature_018 (no treatment-effect heterogeneity by race).
feature_039 effect on pfs_months is consistent across race categories.

analyses.code: OLS with log_f013 * feature_057 product term added to 8-feature model
analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta=+0.0223 (SE 0.0019), t=12, p=3e-33. Significant positive interaction: each step up in feature_057 attenuates the log feature_013 slope by +0.022, so the prognostic information of feature_013 is greatest at lower severity.

analyses.code: OLS with feature_078 * feature_057 product term
analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta=-0.0096 (SE 0.0002), t=-50+, p~0. Strongly significant negative interaction: each severity step reduces the marginal age benefit by 0.0096 months/year of age.

analyses.code: Joint F-test of feature_109 x race_dummies after adjustment
analyses.result_summary: F(4,49983)=0.69, p=0.596. No significant heterogeneity in the feature_109 effect across racial groups; stratified estimates ranged from +0.026 (asian) to +0.253 (black) but the overall interaction is not significant.

analyses.code: Stratified mean differences for feature_039 within each race
analyses.result_summary: Stratified feature_039 effects span -0.27 (other, n1=84) to +0.16 (black, n1=598), with white showing +0.08 (p=0.04). Joint interaction not formally tested as significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_078 has a significant quadratic component beyond its linear effect (the marginal age benefit increases at older ages).
After adjustment, feature_067 (Gleason-like, range 6-10) is associated with shorter pfs_months despite a null univariable effect.
feature_067 is not associated with feature_051 prevalence or with feature_013 levels.
feature_009 has a small significant quadratic component.
feature_016, feature_071, feature_026, feature_096, feature_033, and feature_064 (other ordinal features) have no significant adjusted effect on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: feature_078^2 coefficient = +0.000831 (SE 1.1e-5), t=77, p~0. The age effect is convex: marginal benefit grows with age. Linear age slope drops from +0.174 to +0.066 once the quadratic term is included.
feature_067 adjusted beta = -0.0114/step (SE 0.0015), t=-7.9, p=4.9e-13. Small but significant negative effect (~0.05 months PFS difference between Gleason 6 and 10).

analyses.code: chi2 of feature_067 vs feature_051; spearman of feature_067 vs log_f013
analyses.result_summary: feature_067 vs feature_051: chi^2=1.77, p=0.78. feature_067 vs log feature_013: rho=-0.004, p=0.35. feature_067 is essentially independent of these other prognostic features.
feature_009^2 coefficient = +0.0127 (SE 0.0044), p=3.7e-3. Marginal evidence of mild upward curvature.

analyses.code: OLS for each ordinal feature added to base
analyses.result_summary: feature_016 p=0.58, feature_071 p=0.18, feature_026 p=0.50, feature_096 p=0.36, feature_033 p=0.73, feature_064 p=0.71. None reaches significance after adjustment; their univariable null finding is confirmed.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A 10-feature multivariable model with feature_078, feature_078^2, feature_057, log feature_013, feature_006, feature_009, feature_109, feature_039, feature_092, and feature_067 explains over 97% of the variance in pfs_months.
After fitting the 10-feature model, feature_027 (binary, prevalence 0.60) shows a small positive residual association with pfs_months.
After fitting the 10-feature model, feature_043 (binary, prevalence 0.20) shows a small negative residual association with pfs_months.
After fitting the 10-feature model, feature_022 (binary, prevalence 0.05) shows a small negative residual association with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: R^2 = 0.9736 with 10 features. All coefficients highly significant; the top three predictors (feature_078, feature_057, log feature_013) account for the lion's share of explained variance, with feature_006 and feature_009 adding meaningful but smaller contributions.
feature_027 mean residual difference = +0.0247 (n1=29,962, p=3.2e-20). Persistent small positive association after controlling for the 10-feature model.
feature_043 mean residual difference = -0.0182 (n1=9,973, p=3.9e-11). Small negative residual effect after multivariable adjustment.
feature_022 mean residual difference = -0.0175 (n1=2,493, p=2.5e-4). Statistically significant though modest; feature_022 is not detectable univariably (p=0.57).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Adding feature_027, feature_043, and feature_022 to the 10-feature multivariable model retains all prior coefficient signs/magnitudes and yields R^2 above 0.973, with the new binaries each remaining significant.
feature_027's positive association with pfs_months is detectable only after multivariable adjustment (univariable p>0.25), indicating it is masked by other features in the unadjusted analysis.
feature_022's negative association is also detectable only after adjustment (univariable p=0.57), marking another suppressor relationship.

analyses.result_summary: R^2=0.9737. Coefficients: feature_027 +0.0248 (p=2e-16), feature_043 -0.0184 (p=5.2e-7), feature_022 -0.0180 (p=0.008). All other coefficients essentially unchanged.

Univariable: mean PFS feature_027=0 -> 3.732, =1 -> 3.752, diff +0.020 (p=0.27). Adjusted effect +0.025 (p=2e-16). Confirms suppressor relationship -- feature_027 appears null without controls but becomes a clear positive prognostic indicator after adjustment.
Univariable: mean PFS feature_022=0 -> 3.745, =1 -> 3.722, diff -0.024 (p=0.57). Adjusted: -0.018 (p=0.008). Suppressor pattern -- effect surfaces only with multivariable controls.
